item.title: Re: A small problem
item.content:encoded: I am unsure what you are asking. Can you clarify?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes ,this is good

item.title: A small problem
item.content:encoded:
I would like to ask the author, where did the record of the Chinese Shang Dynasty see?
Please tell me , thank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice article, + 1.

For anyone curious about the code, I actually ran it in an emulator

+ Show gif
- Hide link

[Imgur](https://i.imgur.com/5SiT4xc.gifv)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, whoops!

Thanks for pointing that out, I'll fix it now :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I clicked on 'the info tab' and it says 'SCP-4205: — Programmable Occult', not SCP-2105 and I think the writer should fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After many years, I edited this to add an info box at the top.
Also added attribution to the images used at the very bottom of the article. :]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is amazingly well-put-together.

+1
Congrats on reaching +100! It's a worthy article, you should be proud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know you posted this over a year ago, but I appreciate your thoughts!

It's been so long since I wrote this, I couldn't tell you what my intentions were on that final experiment. I think I just wanted to give some sort of finality to the experiments but still leave a bit of leeway if anyone wanted to add more. But I'm not really sure.

Thank you again! I'm happy you enjoyed it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still around-ish. Just haven't written anything in a while.

I appreciate you fixing things up! Makes me really happy that people are still reading this after all this time.

I plan to come around and fix some things in the article someday, but I haven't really had the chance.

Thanks again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed a lot of minor problems, and the author seems to be absent, so I went through and improved what I could.

+ show block
– hide block
  • Dependant -> dependent
  • Culled a blank line from the rules list. Something about the formatting trick used there (cool btw) made a ghost. Kludged a solution — using ~~~~'s always makes me feel like I'm doing something wrong, but it works.
  • Unfucked Micaelson's notes. The table and the image were competing for space, so I put in empty lines that felt like they should be there anyways and it fixed itself. Made the text less crowded too.
  • Used relative links for the image urls and the experiment log link. Minor thing, but it's less work to do later if the site's URL ever changes.
  • Added a space after the PRINT in the final line.

There's also the matter of the extra spaces in the code for the final, uh, code. Managed to figure out that the author wanted to right-align the numbers and added sufficient spaces for that, but even if they had been rendered properly (via @@'s around the block of spaces), you'd need a monospace font to make it work. Didn't want to implement that myself. Tried to make it happen with DIV blocks but those don't play nice with blockquotes, it seems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the main article. It has a rare mixture of interesting idea and good writing. But. The last experiment, it's just… really don't work for me.
So, we're testing an unknown artifact which realizes wishes of it's users in one form or another and giving it to a technically enslaved criminal, subjected to cruel experiments before, effectively granting him a wish of "revenge". Furthermore all that happens with no additional security and in a Zone with overall lowered security levels. Just one question. What could go right?
Sorry, but personally for me it seems just like a lack of common sence, which stops me from upvoting an overall great article.
P.S. Sorry if my english is bad, my first comment on this wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the .COM filetype generally only functioning on DOS using the COMMAND.COM default program. However, when SCP-2105 is run on other operating systems, it creates its own instance of COMMAND.COM to run inside.

This is, like, almost the exact opposite of how COM files work. I mean, for one thing, COMMAND.COM is, itself, a COM file. That's the only thing that bugs me. I feel the object is very strong overall and it already has my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rituals are highly dependant —> dependent
a through search of W-2105-001's belongings —> thorough

I couldn't make head or tail of DCMT-2105-1 but I found the entry engrossing all the same. Tentative +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only gripe is that DCMT-2105-1 isn't exactly "original work". Having actually written machine code on a C64 (as an infant, surely - how old could I possibly be?), I got really excited to have to reassemble mine track down an emulator. I get why you want to include it, but I think you should have mixed it up a little and made it your own. It's a great place to include an Easter Egg, after all.

Wait, wait - unless it is in fact, your original work. In which case, jeez, I should probably just hand my pinball crown to you.

E: Yeah, the edit date on c2.com certainly leaves ample room for that to be your code. Please ignore my impudence, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made minor fixes of all the typo problems mentioned here and on Reddit. Also changed what COMMAND.COM is referred to so Sensei Le Roof will feel a little less bugged. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I guess COMMAND.COM is technically a file-type for the original DOS OS, but I figured it made the same amount of sense. I can change it if it'd make you feel better. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm immensely bugged by your calling COMMAND.COM an OS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a couple of places where you say "preform" instead of "perform", but other than that, this is pretty good. It's been a while since I've seen a lengthy test log pulled off like that, and I thought the interview was pretty well-done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but only after a through psychological examination

Should be thorough instead of through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a couple of issues with tone and phrasing, but on the whole I enjoyed this and have no issues upvoting.

Rule 4 is currently the primary focus of testing as it has the most baring on the results of the ritual generation after the program settings themselves. For example, it has found if the user's intention is to kill someone they dislike, the resulting ritual will often result in something to cause the target harm. Many D-Class's results were rituals that allowed them to teleport or transport themselves outside the facility.

This paragraph in particular stood out as an issue. First, I believe you are looking for "bearing" instead of "baring". One influences a situation, the other exposes itself to the situation.

Second, the resulting ritual will often result in redundancy. Removing the word resulting will fix that.

Finally, I don't believe teleport is a clinical/scientific term, although I am willing to admit that I could be wrong about that.

Edit: Having just criticised redundancy, I then proceed to use redundant phrasing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is incredibly flattering, thank you. :) Can you tell that I've been lurking SCP since 2008? Because I have. Just finally decided to actually write something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. Not many first-time writers have the chutzpah to pull something this ambitious off, but you managed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is probably one of the best first SCPs I've ever read. This was very well put together.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn son, you put a lot of work into this and it shows. Don't know how you did it, but you kept me hooked through everything, all the way to the end. Great first article man, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good idea, and you executed it amazingly, and you even have a test log. Amazing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it! Old school ritualistic murders combined with modern technology, now take my upvote. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my first SCP. I've spent an inordinate amount of time working on it and got a lot of very useful criticism. I hope you guys enjoy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much Logan! I got a lot of criticism on it and I expect I still will. I'm quite proud of it and spent a lot of time on it, so I hope that's at least apparent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I added a rating module for you.

Also, yay! I remember reading the draft for this and i had some expectations. I told you that this could be a ritualistic 914 and that a nice execution and authenticity would be essential, but i really liked it. Also, i like the formatting. Especially the Experiment Log tables.

I will be giving you my +1.
